title: Self-organizing partially ordered networks
abstract: A system and method for controlling a nodal network. The method includes estimating an effect on the objective caused by the existence or non-existence of a direct connection between a pair of nodes and changing a structure of the nodal network based at least in part on the estimate of the effect. A nodal network includes a strict partially ordered set, a weighted directed acyclic graph, an artificial neural network, and/or a layered feed-forward neural network.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1 . A computer-implemented method for controlling a nodal network comprising a first node and a second node, the first and second nodes comprising activation functions that are evaluatable on a dataset according to an objective defined by an objective function, the method comprising: estimating, by a computer system, an effect on the objective caused by the existence or non-existence of a direct connection between the first node and the second node; and changing, by the computer system, a structure of the nodal network based at least in part on the estimate of the effect. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein estimating the effect on the objective comprises: computing, by the computer system, a weighted average over the dataset of a product of an activation of the first node and a partial derivative of the objective with respect to an input to the second node for each data item in the dataset. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 2, wherein the dataset comprises a development dataset set aside from a training dataset on which the nodal network was trained. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein: the first node and the second node are not directly connected; estimating the effect on the objective comprises estimating, by the computer system, the effect on the objective of adding the direct connection between the first node and the second node; and changing the structure of the nodal network comprises adding, by the computer system, the direct connection between the first node and the second node based at least in part on whether an estimate of the effect on the objective indicates improvement in a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective due to the existence of the direct connection. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein: the first node and the second node are directly connected; 

 estimating the effect on the objective comprises estimating, by the computer system, the effect on the objective of deleting the direct connection between the first node and the second node; and changing the structure of the nodal network comprises deleting, by the computer system, the direct connection between the first node and the second node based at least in part on whether an estimate of the effect on the objective indicates improvement in a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective due to the non-existence of the direct connection. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 5, wherein deleting the direct connection between the first node and the second node is further based at least in part on a magnitude of a connection weight associated with the direct connection between the first node and the second node. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein the nodal network comprises a strict partially ordered set. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein the nodal network comprises a weighted directed acyclic graph. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein the nodal network comprises an artificial neural network. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein the nodal network comprises a layered feed-forward neural network. 1 1 . The computer-implemented method of claim 10, wherein: the first node is located in a first layer of the layered feed-forward neural network and the second node is located in a second layer of the layered feed-forward neural network; and the first node and the second node are not directly connected. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein: the nodal network comprises a first subnetwork and a second subnetwork; the first node is located in the first subnetwork; the second node is located in the second subnetwork; and changing the structure of the nodal network comprises adding, by the computer system, the direct connection from the first node to the second node based at least in part on 

 whether an estimate of the effect on the objective indicates improvement in a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective due to the existence of the direct connection. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein changing the structure of the nodal network is controlled by a machine learning system executed by the computer system. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 13, wherein the machine learning system comprises a learning coach machine learning system. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein: the first node and the second node form a cover pair in a strict partial order corresponding to a transitive closure of the nodal network; and changing the structure of the nodal network comprises deleting a first direct connection from the first node to the second node and adding a second direct connection from the second node to the first node based at least in part on whether an estimate of the effect on the objective indicates improvement in a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective due to replacement of the first direct connection with the second direct connection. 16. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , further comprising: freezing, by the computer system, the direct connection between the first node and the second node such that while the direct connection is frozen a connection weight of the direct connection is not changed during training of the nodal network. 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, wherein the connection weight of the frozen direct connection is zero. 18. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, wherein the connection weight of the frozen direct connection is non-zero. 19. The computer-implemented method of claim 16, further comprising: unfreezing, by the computer system, the frozen direct connection. 20. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein the dataset over which the nodal network is evaluated comprises a full batch of a training dataset. 21. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein: 

 the dataset over which the nodal network is evaluated comprises a mini-batch of a training dataset; and estimating the effect on the objective comprises estimating, by the computer system, a gradient of the objective function for stochastic gradient descent. 22. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein the dataset comprises a first dataset, the method further comprising: assigning, by the computer system, a data influence weight to each data item in the first dataset; training, by the computer system, the nodal network on the first dataset via stochastic gradient descent, which comprises: computing, by the computer system, a weighted average of an estimate of a gradient in each stochastic gradient descent update according to the data influence weight for each data item in the first dataset; measuring, by the computer system during training of the nodal network, a performance of the nodal network on a second dataset; wherein the second dataset is disjoint from the first dataset; and adjusting, by the computer system during training of the nodal network, the data influence weight of one or more data items in the first dataset based on the performance of the nodal network. 23. The computer-implemented method of claim 22, wherein adjusting the data influence weight comprises setting, by the computer system, the data influence weight to zero. 24. The computer-implemented method of claim 22, wherein measuring the performance of the nodal network on the second dataset is semi-supervised. 25. The computer-implemented method of claim 24, wherein semi-supervised measuring of the performance of the nodal network on the second dataset comprises labeling, by the computer system, data items of the second dataset via a recognizer machine learning system. 26. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , further comprising: training, by the computer system, the second node to match an output of the first node. 27. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , further comprising: 

 training, by the computer system, the first node to maximize a magnitude of a correlation between the activation function of the first node and a partial derivative of the objective function with respect to an input to the second node. 28. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein the activation functions comprise non-monotonic activations functions. 29. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein: the nodal network comprises: a first subnetwork for performing a first machine learning task; and a second subnetwork for performing a second machine learning task that is distinct from the first machine learning task; the first node is located in the first subnetwork; and the second node is located in the second subnetwork. 30. The computer-implemented method of claim 29, further comprising: inputting, by the computer system, the dataset to the first subnetwork; and inputting, by the computer system, a subset of the dataset to the second subnetwork. 31. The computer-implemented method of claim 29, further comprising: training, by the computer system, the first subnetwork on the dataset; and training, by the computer system, the second subnetwork on a subset of the dataset. 32. The computer-implemented method of claim 29, further comprising: training, by the computer system, the first subnetwork to distinguish a plurality of categories; and training, by the computer system, the second subnetwork to distinguish a subset oft he plurality of categories. 33. The computer-implemented method of claim 29, wherein the first subnetwork is evaluatable based on a first objective and the second subnetwork is evaluatable based on a second objective, the first objective corresponding to a plurality of categories and the second objective corresponding to a subset of the plurality of categories. 34. The computer-implemented method of claim 29, wherein the first subnetwork and the second subnetwork operate asynchronously, further comprising: 

 monitoring, by the computer system, an individual performance of each of the first subnetwork and the second subnetwork via a machine learning coach executed by the computer system; and changing, by the computer system, the structure of at least one of the first subnetwork or the second subnetwork to improve a combined performance of the first subnetwork and the second subnetwork. 35. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , further comprising: training, by the computer system, the nodal network on the dataset according to the objective; detecting, by the computer system, a problem in a learning process of the nodal network during training of the nodal network via a learning coach machine learning system; and correcting, by the computer system, the problem detected by the learning coach machine learning system. 36. The computer-implemented method of claim 35, wherein detecting the problem in the learning process of the nodal network comprises: detecting, by the computer system, whether a magnitude of an estimated gradient is less than a threshold value across a plurality of stochastic gradient descent updates via the learning coach machine learning system. 37. The computer-implemented method of claim 35, wherein: the dataset comprises a first dataset; and detecting the problem in the learning process of the nodal network comprises detecting, by the computer system, whether a difference between a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective on the first dataset and the performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective on a second dataset that is disjoint from the first dataset exceeds a threshold value via the learning coach machine learning system. 38. The computer-implemented method of claim 35, wherein detecting the problem in the learning process of the nodal network comprises: detecting, by the computer system, whether the nodal network misclassifies a particular data item of the dataset over a plurality of training epochs via the learning coach machine learning system. 39. The computer-implemented method of claim 35, wherein detecting the problem in the learning process of the nodal network comprises: detecting, by the computer system via the learning coach machine learning system, whether the nodal network classifies a plurality of data items of the dataset into a single category, wherein the classified plurality of data items are designated to be classified into a plurality of categories. 40. The computer-implemented method of claim 35, wherein detecting the problem in the learning process of the nodal network comprises: detecting, by the computer system, whether a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective on the dataset is worse than the performance of an ensemble of nodal networks by an amount exceeding a criterion. 41. The computer-implemented method of claim 35, wherein correcting the problem detected by the learning coach machine learning system comprises: adding or deleting, by the computer system, a connection between the first node and the second node. 42. The computer-implemented method of claim 35, wherein correcting the problem detected by the learning coach machine learning system comprises: unfreezing, by the computer system, a connection weight between the first node and the second node. 43. The computer-implemented method of claim 35, wherein correcting the problem detected by the learning coach machine learning system comprises: copying, by the computer system, the nodal network into a plurality of copy nodal networks; wherein the plurality of copy nodal networks each comprise a first set of copy nodes that are copies of nodes of the nodal network that are connected to input nodes of the nodal network; wherein the plurality of copy nodal networks each comprise a second set of copy nodes that are copies of nodes of the nodal network that are connected to output nodes of the nodal network; combining, by the computer system, the plurality of copy nodal networks into a combined nodal network; connecting, by the computer system, each of the first set of copy nodes to respective input nodes of the combined nodal network; and 

 connecting, by the computer system, each of the second set of copy nodes to respective output nodes of the combined nodal network. 44. The computer-implemented method of claim 43, wherein the plurality of copy nodal networks each comprise copy inner layer nodes that are copies of inner layer nodes of the nodal network, the method further comprising: initializing, by the computer system, the combined nodal network such that the copy inner layer nodes of each of the plurality of copy nodal networks are not connected to the copy inner layer nodes of another of the plurality of copy nodal networks; and adding or deleting, by the computer system, connections between the copy inner layer nodes from different copy nodal networks via the learning coach machine learning system; wherein the learning coach machine learning system has been trained to estimate the effect on the objective for the combined nodal network resulting from adding or deleting the connections between the copy inner layer nodes. 45. The computer-implemented method of claim 44, further comprising: delineating, by the computer system, a strict partial order amongst the plurality of copy nodal networks; determining, by the computer system, whether a first node of a first copy nodal network of the plurality of copy nodal networks is less than or incomparable to a second node of a second copy nodal network of the plurality of copy nodal networks; and adding, by the computer system, a connection from the first node to the second node only if the first node is less than or incomparable to the second node. 46. The computer-implemented method of claim 45, wherein the delineated strict partial order is a total order. 47. The computer-implemented method of claim 45, further comprising: positioning, by the computer system, the first copy nodal network and the second copy nodal network in the strict partial order such that they are comparable. 48. The computer-implemented method of claim 45, further comprising: positioning, by the computer system, the first copy nodal network at a first position in the strict partial order; and positioning, by the computer system, the second copy nodal network at a second position in the strict partial order; 

 wherein the first position is lower than the second position. 49. The computer-implemented method of claim 44, wherein the nodal network comprises a first nodal network, the method further comprising: training, by the computer system, a second nodal network to perform a discrimination task that has not been learned by the first nodal network. 50. The computer-implemented method of claim 44, further comprising: training, by the computer system, the nodal network via one-shot learning. 51. The computer-implemented method of claim 44, further comprising: training, by the computer system, the nodal network to discriminate between a first data item from the dataset and a second data item from the dataset. 52. The computer-implemented method of claim 44, wherein the nodal network comprises a first nodal network, the method further comprising: training, by the computer system, a second nodal network to perform a machine learning task that is not performed by the first nodal network. 53. A computer system for controlling a nodal network, the computer system comprising: a processor; and a memory coupled to the processor, the memory storing: the nodal network comprising a first node and a second node, the first and second nodes comprising activation functions that are evaluatable on a dataset according to an objective defined by an objective function; and instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the computer system to: estimate an effect on the objective caused by the existence or nonexistence of a direct connection between the first node and the second node; and change a structure of the nodal network based at least in part on whether the direct connection between the first node and the second node exists. 54. The computer system of claim 53, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to estimate the effect on the objective by computing a weighted average over the dataset of a product of an activation of the first node and a partial derivative of the objective with respect to an input to the second node for each data item in the dataset. 55. The computer system of claim 54, wherein the dataset comprises a development dataset set aside from a training dataset on which the nodal network was trained. 56. The computer system of claim 53, wherein: the first node and the second node are not directly connected; and the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to: estimate the effect on the objective by estimating the effect on the objective of adding the direct connection between the first node and the second node; and change the structure of the nodal network by adding the direct connection between the first node and the second node based at least in part on whether the estimate of the effect on the objective indicates improvement in a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective due to the existence of the direct connection. 57. The computer system of claim 53, wherein: the first node and the second node are directly connected; the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to estimate the effect on the objective by estimating the effect on the objective of deleting the direct connection between the first node and the second node; and change the structure of the nodal network by deleting the direct connection between the first node and the second node based at least in part on whether an estimate of the effect on the objective indicates improvement in a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective due to the non-existence of the direct connection. 58. The computer system of claim 57, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to delete the direct connection between the first node and the second node further based at least in part on a magnitude of a connection weight associated with the direct connection between the first node and the second node. 59. The computer system of claim 53, wherein the nodal network comprises at least one of a strict partially ordered set, a weighted directed acyclic graph, an artificial neural network, a layered feed-forward neural network. 60. The computer system of claim 59, wherein: the nodal network comprises the layered feed-forward neural network; the first node is located in a first layer of the layered feed-forward neural network and the second node is located in a second layer of the layered feed-forward neural network; and 

 the first node and the second node are not directly connected. 61. The computer system of claim 53, wherein: the nodal network comprises a first subnetwork and a second subnetwork; the first node is located in the first subnetwork; the second node is located in the second subnetwork; and the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to change the structure of the nodal network by adding the direct connection from the first node to the second node based at least in part on whether an estimate of the effect on the objective indicates improvement in a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective due to the existence of the direct connection. 62. The computer system of claim 53, wherein: the memory stores a machine learning system for controlling the nodal network; and the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to change the structure of the nodal network according to the machine learning system. 63. The computer system claim 62, wherein the machine learning system comprises a learning coach machine learning system. 64. The computer system of claim 53, wherein: the first node and the second node form a cover pair in a strict partial order corresponding to a transitive closure of the nodal network; and the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to change the structure of the nodal network by deleting a first direct connection from the first node to the second node and adding a second direct connection from the second node to the first node based at least in part on whether an estimate of the effect on the objective indicates improvement in a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective due to replacement of the first direct connection with the second direct connection. 65. The computer system of claim 53, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to freeze the direct connection between the first node and the second node such that while the direct conneciton is frozen a connection weight of the direct connection is not changed during training of the nodal network. 66. The computer system of claim 65, wherein the connection weight of the frozen direct connection is zero. 67. The computer system of claim 65, wherein the connection weight of the frozen direct connection is non-zero. 68. The computer system of claim 53, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to unfreeze the frozen direct connection. 69. The computer system of claim 53, wherein the dataset over which the nodal network is evaluated comprises a full batch of a training dataset. 70. The computer system of claim 53, wherein: the dataset over which the nodal network is evaluated comprises a mini-batch of a training dataset; and the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to estimate the effect on the objective by estimating a gradient of the objective function for stochastic gradient descent. 71. The computer system of claim 53, wherein: the dataset comprises a first dataset; and the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to: assign a data influence weight to each data item in the first dataset; train the nodal network on the first dataset via stochastic gradient descent, which comprises: computing a weighted average of an estimate of a gradient in each stochastic gradient descent update according to the data influence weight for each data item in the first dataset; measure, during training of the nodal network, a performance of the nodal network on a second dataset; wherein the second dataset is disjoint from the first dataset; and adjust, during training of the nodal network, the data influence weight of one or more data items in the first dataset based on the performance of the nodal network. 72. The computer system of claim 71 , wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to adjust the data influence weight by setting the data influence weight to zero. 73. The computer system of claim 71 , wherein the performance of the nodal network on the second dataset comprises is measured via semi-supervised machine learning. 74. The computer system of claim 73, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to label data items of the second dataset via a recognizer machine learning system for the semi-supervised machine learning. 75. The computer system of claim 53, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to train the second node to match an output of the first node. 76. The computer system of claim 53, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to train the first node to maximize a magnitude of a correlation between the activation function of the first node and a partial derivative of the objective function with respect to an input to the second node. 77. The computer system of claim 53, wherein the activation functions comprise nonmonotonic activations functions. 78. The computer system of claim 53, wherein: the nodal network comprises: a first subnetwork for performing a first machine learning task; and a second subnetwork for performing a second machine learning task that is distinct from the first machine learning task; the first node is located in the first subnetwork; and the second node is located in the second subnetwork. 79. The computer system of claim 78, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to: input the dataset to the first subnetwork; and input a subset of the dataset to the second subnetwork. 80. The computer system of claim 78, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to: train the first subnetwork on the dataset; and train the second subnetwork on a subset of the dataset. 81. The computer system of claim 78, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to: train the first subnetwork to distinguish a plurality of categories; and train the second subnetwork to distinguish a subset oft he plurality of categories. 82. The computer system of claim 78, wherein the first subnetwork is evaluatable based on a first objective and the second subnetwork is evaluatable based on a second objective, the first objective corresponding to a plurality of categories and the second objective corresponding to a subset of the plurality of categories. 83. The computer system of claim 78, wherein: the first subnetwork and the second subnetwork operate asynchronously; and the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to: monitor an individual performance of each of the first subnetwork and the second subnetwork via a machine learning coach executed by the computer system; and change the structure of at least one of the first subnetwork or the second subnetwork to improve a combined performance of the first subnetwork and the second subnetwork. 84. The computer system of claim 53, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to: train the nodal network on the dataset according to the objective; detect a problem in a learning process of the nodal network during training of the nodal network via a learning coach machine learning system; and correct the problem detected by the learning coach machine learning system. 85. The computer system of claim 84, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to detect the problem in the learning process of the nodal network by detecting whether a magnitude of an estimated gradient is less than a threshold value across a plurality of stochastic gradient descent updates via the learning coach machine learning system. 86. The computer system of claim 84, wherein: the dataset comprises a first dataset; and the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to detect the problem in the learning process of the nodal network by detecting whether a difference between a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective on the first dataset and the 

 performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective on a second dataset that is disjoint from the first dataset exceeds a threshold value via the learning coach machine learning system. 87. The computer system of claim 84, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to detect the problem in the learning process of the nodal network by detecting whether the nodal network misclassifies a particular data item of the dataset over a plurality of training epochs via the learning coach machine learning system. 88. The computer system of claim 84, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to detect the problem in the learning process of the nodal network by detecting, via the learning coach machine learning system, whether the nodal network classifies a plurality of data items of the dataset into a single category, wherein the classified plurality of data items are designated to be classified into a plurality of categories. 89. The computer system of claim 84, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to detect the problem in the learning process of the nodal network by detecting whether a performance of the nodal network with respect to the objective on the dataset is worse than the performance of an ensemble of nodal networks by an amount exceeding a criterion. 90. The computer system of claim 84, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to correct the problem detected by the learning coach machine learning system by adding or deleting a connection between the first node and the second node. 91. The computer system of claim 84, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to correct the problem detected by the learning coach machine learning system by unfreezing a connection weight between the first node and the second node. 92. The computer system of claim 84, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to correct the problem detected by the learning coach machine learning system by: copying the nodal network into a plurality of copy nodal networks; 

 wherein the plurality of copy nodal networks each comprise a first set of copy nodes that are copies of nodes of the nodal network that are connected to input nodes of the nodal network; wherein the plurality of copy nodal networks each comprise a second set of copy nodes that are copies of nodes of the nodal network that are connected to output nodes of the nodal network; combining the plurality of copy nodal networks into a combined nodal network; connecting each of the first set of copy nodes to respective input nodes of the combined nodal network; and connecting each of the second set of copy nodes to respective output nodes of the combined nodal network. 93. The computer system of claim 92, wherein: the plurality of copy nodal networks each comprise copy inner layer nodes that are copies of inner layer nodes of the nodal network: and the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to: initialize the combined nodal network such that the copy inner layer nodes of each of the plurality of copy nodal networks are not connected to the copy inner layer nodes of another of the plurality of copy nodal networks; and add or delete connections between the copy inner layer nodes from different copy nodal networks via the learning coach machine learning system; wherein the learning coach machine learning system has been trained to estimate the effect on the objective for the combined nodal network resulting from adding or deleting the connections between the copy inner layer nodes. 94. The computer system of claim 93, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to: delineate a strict partial order amongst the plurality of copy nodal networks; determine whether a first node of a first copy nodal network of the plurality of copy nodal networks is less than or incomparable to a second node of a second copy nodal network of the plurality of copy nodal networks; and add a connection from the first node to the second node only if the first node is less than or incomparable to the second node. 95. The computer system of claim 94, wherein the delineated strict partial order is a total order. 96. The computer system of claim 94, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to position the first copy nodal network and the second copy nodal network in the strict partial order such that they are comparable. 97. The computer system of claim 94, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to: position the first copy nodal network at a first position in the strict partial order; and position the second copy nodal network at a second position in the strict partial order; wherein the first position is lower than the second position. 98. The computer system of claim 93, wherein: the nodal network comprises a first nodal network; and the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to train a second nodal network to perform a discrimination task that has not been learned by the first nodal network. 99. The computer system of claim 93, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to train the nodal network via one-shot learning. 100. The computer system of claim 93, wherein the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to train the nodal network to discriminate between a first data item from the dataset and a second data item from the dataset. 101. The computer system of claim 93, wherein: the nodal network comprises a first nodal network; and the memory stores instructions that cause the computer system to train a second nodal network to perform a machine learning task that is not performed by the first nodal network.
